questions.prompt:
A researcher studies how sleep interruption affects memory in adult volunteers. Group A sleeps for 8 hours without interruption. Group B is awakened briefly every 90 minutes but still spends 8 hours in bed. The next morning, both groups complete the same word-recall test.
Group | Average words recalled out of 30
A | 24
B | 17
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Sleep interruption was associated with lower recall scores.
choices.B: Sleep interruption increased recall scores.
choices.C: Both groups had the same average recall score.
choices.D: Memory cannot be measured with a recall test.

questions.correctAnswerText: Sleep interruption was associated with lower recall scores.
questions.explanation: Group B had interrupted sleep and recalled fewer words on average than Group A.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Potential energy can be calculated as weight × height.
Object | Weight (N) | Height above floor (m)
A | 20 | 4
B | 35 | 2
C | 12 | 8
D | 50 | 1
Which object has the greatest gravitational potential energy?

choices.A: Object A
choices.B: Object B
choices.C: Object C

questions.correctAnswerText: Object C
questions.explanation: Potential energy = weight × height. Object C has 12 N × 8 m = 96 J, the greatest value listed.

questions.prompt:
A region has large deposits of copper ore. Mining copper can provide material for electrical wiring and electronics, but it can also disturb land, use water, and produce waste rock. Recycling copper from old wiring can reduce the need for new mining.
Which statement best describes one benefit of recycling copper?

choices.A: It can reduce the amount of new copper ore that must be mined.
choices.B: It creates unlimited copper atoms from sunlight.
choices.C: It prevents all minerals from forming naturally.
choices.D: It makes copper unnecessary in electrical devices.

questions.correctAnswerText: It can reduce the amount of new copper ore that must be mined.
questions.explanation: Recycling copper can reduce demand for newly mined copper ore.

questions.prompt:
The endocrine system helps maintain blood glucose levels. When blood glucose rises after a meal, the pancreas releases insulin. Insulin helps cells take in glucose and helps the liver store glucose as glycogen. When blood glucose falls, the pancreas releases glucagon, which signals the liver to release glucose.
Which idea is best illustrated by this process?

choices.A: Hormones help regulate internal conditions through feedback.
choices.B: Hormones prevent cells from using glucose.
choices.C: The pancreas stores all oxygen for the body.
choices.D: Blood glucose never changes after meals.

questions.correctAnswerText: Hormones help regulate internal conditions through feedback.
questions.explanation: Insulin and glucagon help keep blood glucose within a normal range through feedback.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A 40 N force moves a crate 6 meters across a smooth floor.
Work = force × distance
How much work is done on the crate in joules?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Work = 40 N × 6 m = 240 J.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student tests how the length of a lever arm affects the input force needed to lift a 100 N load. The student changes the distance from the fulcrum to where the input force is applied. The same load and same lever are used in each trial.
Which TWO factors should be kept constant?

choices.A: Weight of the load
choices.B: Type of lever used
choices.C: Length of the input lever arm

questions.correctAnswerText: Weight of the load | Type of lever used
questions.explanation: The load and lever should stay the same so lever-arm length is the main variable being tested.

questions.prompt: A child inherits one allele for normal hemoglobin and one allele for sickle-cell hemoglobin. Both forms of hemoglobin can be present in the blood. This inheritance pattern is an example of [D1], and the alleles affect the structure of a [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=codominance; D2=protein
questions.explanation: Both hemoglobin alleles can be expressed, and hemoglobin is a protein.

questions.prompt:
A student tests an unknown liquid with universal indicator paper. The paper turns orange-red, which the indicator chart shows as pH 4. The student then adds the liquid to a small amount of baking soda, and bubbles form.
Which conclusion is best supported?

choices.A: The liquid is acidic.
choices.B: The liquid is strongly basic.
choices.C: The liquid is pure oxygen gas.

questions.correctAnswerText: The liquid is acidic.
questions.explanation: pH 4 and bubbling with baking soda are consistent with an acidic liquid.

questions.prompt:
A climate scientist compares two gases. Nitrogen makes up most of Earth’s atmosphere but absorbs very little infrared radiation. Methane is present in much smaller amounts but absorbs infrared radiation strongly. Infrared absorption by gases can trap heat in the atmosphere.
Which statement is best supported by this information?

choices.A: Methane can influence atmospheric warming even at lower concentrations.
choices.B: Nitrogen absorbs all infrared radiation from Earth.
choices.C: Methane cannot interact with infrared radiation.
choices.D: Atmospheric gases have no effect on heat transfer.

questions.correctAnswerText: Methane can influence atmospheric warming even at lower concentrations.
questions.explanation: Methane absorbs infrared radiation strongly, so it can affect heat trapping even in smaller amounts.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A doctor records thyroid hormone level and thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) level in four patients.
Patient | Thyroid hormone | TSH level
A | Normal | Normal
B | Low | High
C | High | Low
D | Normal | Normal
In a typical negative feedback loop, low thyroid hormone can lead to higher TSH release.
Which patient’s results best match low thyroid hormone causing increased TSH?

choices.A: Patient A
choices.B: Patient B
choices.C: Patient C

questions.correctAnswerText: Patient B
questions.explanation: Patient B has low thyroid hormone and high TSH, matching the described negative feedback response.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.A: Angle at which the ramp is set
tiles.B: Time for the cart to travel 1 meter
tiles.C: Same cart used in every trial

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Angle at which the ramp is set; T2=Time for the cart to travel 1 meter; T3=Same cart used in every trial
questions.explanation: Ramp angle is changed, travel time is measured, and the same cart is controlled.

questions.prompt:
The liver can convert excess glucose into glycogen for storage. Later, when blood glucose is low, the liver can break down glycogen and release glucose into the blood. This helps provide fuel for body cells between meals.
Which function of the liver is described?

choices.A: Helping regulate blood glucose storage and release
choices.B: Exchanging oxygen and carbon dioxide in air sacs
choices.C: Sending electrical impulses through neurons

questions.correctAnswerText: Helping regulate blood glucose storage and release
questions.explanation: The liver stores glucose as glycogen and releases glucose when needed.

questions.prompt:
A roller coaster car is at the top of a hill. As it moves downward, its height decreases and its speed increases. Some gravitational potential energy changes into kinetic energy as the car moves.
Which energy change is mainly described?

choices.A: Gravitational potential energy changing into kinetic energy
choices.B: Electrical energy changing into nuclear energy
choices.C: Chemical energy changing into sound only

questions.correctAnswerText: Gravitational potential energy changing into kinetic energy
questions.explanation: As height decreases and speed increases, gravitational potential energy changes into kinetic energy.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A town reduces its annual landfill waste from 8,000 tons to 6,400 tons after starting a recycling program.
What is the percent decrease in landfill waste?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: Waste decreased by 1,600 tons. 1,600 ÷ 8,000 × 100 = 20%.

questions.prompt:
A researcher studies whether a balance-training program reduces fall risk in older adults. Group A completes balance exercises for 8 weeks. Group B continues normal activity without the program. Both groups take the same balance test before and after the study.
Why is Group B included?

choices.A: It provides a comparison group for evaluating the training program.
choices.B: It guarantees that Group A will improve.
choices.C: It prevents researchers from collecting data.
choices.D: It changes normal activity into balance training.

questions.correctAnswerText: It provides a comparison group for evaluating the training program.
questions.explanation: The comparison group helps show whether changes are related to the training program.

questions.prompt:
A student compares how the mass of a swinging pendulum affects the time for one swing. The student tests 50 g, 100 g, and 150 g masses. The string length and release angle are kept the same.
What is the independent variable?

choices.A: Mass of the pendulum bob
choices.B: Time for one swing
choices.C: String length

questions.correctAnswerText: Mass of the pendulum bob
questions.explanation: The mass is the factor intentionally changed in the experiment.

questions.prompt: A compound machine is made from two or more simple machines. A wheelbarrow uses a wheel and axle plus a lever. The wheel and axle helps reduce [D1], while the handles act as a [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=friction; D2=lever
questions.explanation: A wheel and axle can reduce friction, and wheelbarrow handles act as a lever.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A physical therapist measures grip strength before and after a hand-strengthening program.
Participant group | Average grip strength before (kg) | Average grip strength after (kg)
Group A | 22 | 29
Group B | 21 | 22
Which claim is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Group A showed a greater increase in average grip strength.
choices.B: Group B showed the greater increase in average grip strength.
choices.C: Both groups decreased in average grip strength.

questions.correctAnswerText: Group A showed a greater increase in average grip strength.
questions.explanation: Group A increased by 7 kg, while Group B increased by 1 kg.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student records the input force needed to move the same heavy box using different simple machines.
Simple machine setup | Input force needed (N)
No machine | 300
Inclined plane | 180
Pulley system | 120
Lever system | 90
Which setup reduced the input force the most?

choices.A: No machine
choices.B: Inclined plane
choices.C: Pulley system

questions.correctAnswerText: Lever system
questions.explanation: The lever system required the least input force, 90 N, so it reduced input force the most.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A city compares two building materials for outdoor walkways.
Material | Sunlight reflected (%) | Surface temperature after 4 sunny hours (°C)
Dark brick | 12 | 49
Light stone | 46 | 34
Which TWO statements are best supported by the data?

choices.A: Light stone reflected a higher percentage of sunlight than dark brick.
choices.B: Dark brick reached a higher surface temperature than light stone.
choices.C: Dark brick reflected more sunlight than light stone.
choices.D: Both materials reached exactly the same temperature.
choices.E: Light stone had the higher surface temperature.

questions.correctAnswerText: Light stone reflected a higher percentage of sunlight than dark brick. | Dark brick reached a higher surface temperature than light stone.
questions.explanation: Light stone reflected 46% and reached 34°C, while dark brick reflected 12% and reached 49°C.

questions.prompt:
A nerve impulse reaches a muscle cell. Calcium ions are released inside the muscle cell, allowing protein fibers to slide past each other. This sliding shortens the muscle cell and produces contraction.
Which process is mainly described?

choices.A: Muscle contraction
choices.B: Rock compaction
choices.C: Solar radiation

questions.correctAnswerText: Muscle contraction
questions.explanation: Sliding protein fibers shorten muscle cells, producing muscle contraction.

questions.prompt:
A student observes a skateboard rolling across a flat surface. The skateboard slows down and stops because friction and air resistance act opposite the direction of motion.
Which statement best explains why the skateboard stops?

choices.A: Unbalanced forces act opposite the skateboard’s motion.
choices.B: Balanced forces make the skateboard speed up forever.
choices.C: Gravity stops existing on flat surfaces.
choices.D: Motion cannot change unless the object is in water.

questions.correctAnswerText: Unbalanced forces act opposite the skateboard’s motion.
questions.explanation: Friction and air resistance oppose motion and slow the skateboard.

questions.prompt:
A geologist describes how limestone can form from the shells of marine organisms. Over long periods, shell fragments accumulate on the seafloor, become compacted, and are cemented together into rock.
Which rock type is most likely formed by this process?

choices.A: Sedimentary rock
choices.B: Igneous rock
choices.C: Metamorphic rock only

questions.correctAnswerText: Sedimentary rock
questions.explanation: Compacted and cemented shell fragments can form sedimentary limestone.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A clinic screens 280 adults for low vitamin D levels. Of these adults, 84 have low vitamin D levels.
What percent of the adults have low vitamin D levels?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.

A student wants to test how surface texture affects how far a hockey puck slides. The student slides the same puck with the same starting push across smooth plastic, wood, and rough rubber.
Which factor should be measured as the dependent variable?

choices.A: Distance the puck slides
choices.B: Type of surface texture
choices.C: Starting push, kept the same
choices.D: Type of puck, kept the same

questions.correctAnswerText: Distance the puck slides
questions.explanation: The dependent variable is the measured result after changing surface texture.

questions.prompt:
A kidney nephron filters small molecules from blood. Useful substances such as glucose and much of the water are reabsorbed back into the blood, while extra wastes remain in the fluid that becomes urine.
Which statement best describes one function of nephrons?

choices.A: They filter blood and help form urine.
choices.B: They pump air into the lungs.
choices.C: They make sunlight into sugars.

questions.correctAnswerText: They filter blood and help form urine.
questions.explanation: Nephrons filter blood, reabsorb useful substances, and help form urine.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures the stopping distance of the same bicycle at different starting speeds on the same dry road.
Starting speed (m/s): 2, 4, 6, 8
Stopping distance (m): 1.5, 6.0, 13.5, 24.0
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Stopping distance increased as starting speed increased.
choices.B: Stopping distance decreased as starting speed increased.
choices.C: Stopping distance stayed constant for all speeds.
choices.D: The bicycle stopped instantly at the highest speed.

questions.correctAnswerText: Stopping distance increased as starting speed increased.
questions.explanation: The stopping distance rose from 1.5 m to 24.0 m as starting speed increased.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares four stars.
Star | Color | Surface temperature (K)
A | Red | 3,200
B | Yellow | 5,800
C | White | 9,500
D | Blue | 18,000
Which star has the highest surface temperature?

choices.A: Star A
choices.B: Star B
choices.C: Star C

questions.correctAnswerText: Star D
questions.explanation: Star D has the highest listed surface temperature, 18,000 K.

questions.prompt:
In a forest, some fungi grow in association with tree roots. The fungal network can help trees absorb minerals, while trees provide sugars to the fungi. In dry years, trees connected to these fungi may maintain better mineral uptake.
Which ecological relationship is described?

choices.A: Mutualism
choices.B: Predation
choices.C: Competition

questions.correctAnswerText: Mutualism
questions.explanation: Both fungi and trees benefit from the relationship.

questions.prompt:
A student compares two materials. Material A is easily shaped into thin sheets without breaking. Material B breaks into pieces when hammered. The ability to be shaped into sheets is called malleability.
Which statement is best supported?

choices.A: Material A is more malleable than Material B.
choices.B: Material B is more malleable than Material A.
choices.C: Both materials must be gases.
choices.D: Malleability means the same thing as magnetism.

questions.correctAnswerText: Material A is more malleable than Material B.
questions.explanation: Material A can be shaped into sheets, while Material B breaks when hammered.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A researcher tests whether a new ankle exercise improves balance after a sprain. Participants are assigned to an exercise group or a usual-care group. Balance is measured using the same test after four weeks.
Which TWO choices would improve the investigation?

choices.A: Use the same balance test for all participants.
choices.B: Include enough participants in both groups.
choices.C: Change the balance test for each participant.
choices.D: Record only results from participants who improved.
choices.E: Give only one group extra testing time.

questions.correctAnswerText: Use the same balance test for all participants. | Include enough participants in both groups.
questions.explanation: A consistent test and adequate sample size improve reliability.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures the frequency and wavelength of several waves traveling at the same speed.
Wave | Frequency (Hz) | Wavelength (m)
A | 5 | 12
B | 10 | 6
C | 15 | 4
D | 20 | 3
Which wave has a speed of 60 m/s?

choices.A: Wave A only
choices.B: Waves A and B only
choices.C: Waves A, B, C, and D

questions.correctAnswerText: Waves A, B, C, and D
questions.explanation: Wave speed = frequency × wavelength. Each wave has speed 60 m/s.

questions.prompt:
A DNA sequence in a gene changes from GAA to GTA. This change leads to a different amino acid being placed in a protein. The altered protein does not bind its target molecule as well as the original protein.
Which statement best explains the result?

choices.A: A gene mutation can change a protein and affect how it works.
choices.B: DNA changes can never affect proteins.
choices.C: Proteins are made of planets and minerals.
choices.D: All gene mutations remove chromosomes completely.

questions.correctAnswerText: A gene mutation can change a protein and affect how it works.
questions.explanation: A DNA sequence change can alter an amino acid and affect protein function.

questions.prompt:
After a severe drought, a pond becomes a meadow. Over time, grasses, shrubs, and then young trees begin growing in the area. The community changes gradually as soil conditions and available resources change.
Which ecological process is described?

choices.A: Ecological succession
choices.B: Static electricity
choices.C: Boiling

questions.correctAnswerText: Ecological succession
questions.explanation: Succession is the gradual change in a biological community over time.
